tip: Always ask about total comp: stipends, housing, and relocation matter. Many FAANG internships pay $40–50/hr plus housing.
Base salary is just the start. Year-end bonuses at bulge-bracket banks can be 50–100% of base. Negotiate your start date and signing bonus.
SQL and Python skills significantly boost intern comp. Certifications in Tableau or Power BI can increase your rate by $5–8/hr.
PM intern pay varies widely. At top tech companies expect $35–55/hr. Startup PMs often earn less cash but gain more responsibility and equity.
At MBB firms (McKinsey, BCG, Bain), first-year analysts earn $85–90k base. Expect significant travel stipends on top of base pay.
Marketing pay varies by company size. Negotiate by highlighting social or content portfolio. Remote roles at tech companies pay 20–40% more.
Government and defense cybersecurity internships require clearance but pay premium rates. Private sector roles reward certifications like CompTIA Security+.
FP&A internships at large corporations pay $18–28/hr. Excel modeling skills are your biggest leverage point for comp negotiation.
Business ops roles at startups often come with equity. Ask about vesting schedules and what a realistic exit looks like before accepting.
Portfolio quality drives pay more than GPA for design roles. A strong Figma portfolio with 2–3 case studies can add $5–10/hr to your rate.
